rationale: Both candidates correctly validate the core migration logic (delegated loaders rely on outer AutoWeightsLoader, direct loaders apply the mapper explicitly) and raise a similar valid concern about the delegated-loader test not proving the outer mapping actually fires. X is cleaner and more grounded, citing exact upstream file:line evidence, and crucially flags that `hunyuan_image3_transformer.py` was dropped from `_STALE_API_FILES` and shipped unfixed — a direct hit on the latent gap (later issue #4891) and the ground truth's 'coordinates with #4808 to cover all 5 call sites' note. Y instead explicitly reasons this file 'has only a comment, no broken code' — actively dismissing the real gap — and its output is garbled/truncated with a dubious fabricated-looking citation ('upstream vLLM PR #43167'), hurting precision and actionability.
